title: Troilus and Criseyde
summary: The repository XML is a local copy of University of Michigan CME item CME00021, an encoding of Frederick J. Furnivall's 1894–1895 Chaucer Society edition Three More Parallel Texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde. The edition aligns independent transcriptions of St John's College Cambridge MS L.1, Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 61, and British Library Harley MS 1239; Furnivall fills explicitly marked gaps in the Harley 1239 column from British Library Additional MS 12044 and one stanza from Harley MS 2392. The XML serializes the three printed columns as sequential witness-version divisions and is incomplete at the end: it stops at Book V line 1834 on printed page 294 and omits printed pages 295–296, lines 1835–1869, in all three streams. A complete Google Books scan of the bound edition and a complete 326-canvas Parker Library IIIF surrogate of Corpus MS 61 are cached. No public complete digital surrogate was verified for St John's L.1 or the three British Library manuscripts on 2026-07-11; complete-object photography is requestable, subject to repository terms, condition, and fees.
review_notes: The five manuscript entities are textual sources for the edition, but only three are its principal parallel columns; Additional MS 12044 and Harley MS 2392 supply explicitly marked passages in the composite Harley 1239 column.
A complete physical-object reproduction is not the same as the work portion. Work folios/pages/canvases are mapped separately in each access or relation record.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00021
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00021.
The file contains 2 DIV1, 5 DIV2, 15 DIV3, 3,507 LG, 24,542 L, 884 PB, 11,066 MILESTONE, and 253 NOTE elements.
The encoded text ends three streams at Book V line 1834 although the source edition continues through line 1869.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00021
entities.description: A public structured digital text representing the title page and most of Furnivall's three parallel witness transcriptions and notes.
notes: The public item page identifies the 1894–1895 edition and applies No Copyright–United States.
Comparison with the scan shows that the encoding omits nearly all preliminary matter, Macaulay's note on Benoît, and the final two printed pages of the poem.

entities.label: Furnivall, Three More Parallel Texts, 1894–1895
alternate_labels: Three More Parallel Texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde
entities.description: A two-part Chaucer Society parallel-text edition whose bound title leaf dates the parts 1894–1895 and whose copying-and-collation note is dated 13 February 1896.

date_statements.label: publication
date_statements.notes: The combined scan includes Furnivall's copying-and-collation note dated 13 February 1896; the exact issue history of the bound title leaf remains unresolved.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Three More Parallel Texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde, from MS. L.1 in St. John's College, Cambridge; MS. No. 61 in Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; and the Harleian MS. 1239 in the British Museum, with a Note on Chaucer's Borrowings from Benoît de Sainte-More by G. C. Macaulay, Chaucer Society, First Series 87–88, 2 parts (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner; Oxford: Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1894–1895).
bibliographic.title: Three More Parallel Texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner; Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press

notes: A. Rogers copied and collated the St John's and Corpus manuscripts; Furnivall copied the Harley manuscript.
Macaulay's separately headed note on Chaucer's borrowings from Benoît de Sainte-More is edition-level supplementary scholarship and is not an intrinsic part of Troilus and Criseyde.
The Harley 1239 column is composite where bracketed or noted supplied readings come from Additional MS 12044 or Harley MS 2392.

entities.label: St John's College, Cambridge, MS L.1
entities.description: A parchment codex containing Troilus and Criseyde on fols. 1r–119v, followed by Robert Henryson's Testament of Cresseid in a later hand.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.notes: The Late Medieval English Scribes record gives 1425–1450; James's older catalog uses a broader fifteenth/sixteenth-century description.

physical_description.support: Parchment, with paper and parchment flyleaves
physical_description.extent: Three paper flyleaves, one parchment flyleaf, fols. 1–128, and three paper flyleaves according to Late Medieval English Scribes
notes: Troilus and Criseyde ends on fol. 119v; the later Testament of Cresseid occupies the remainder.
Acquired by St John's College in 1683; the principal scribe is unidentified.
No public whole-codex digital surrogate was found on 2026-07-11.
The online Late Medieval English Scribes record exposes selected scholarly images but no explicit open-reuse license was found, so no selected image was cached.

entities.label: Corpus Christi College, Cambridge, MS 61
entities.description: A luxury parchment copy of Troilus and Criseyde, planned for more than ninety images but completed with only the full-page frontispiece on fol. 1v.

physical_description.extent: 151 folios plus two; foliation a–d, i–ii, 1–151, e–h
notes: Five seven-line stanzas per page.
The poem occupies fols. 1v–150r; the painted frontispiece is on 1v, text begins on 2r, and the rubric Explicit liber Troily appears on 150r.
Fol. 150v records ownership and fol. 151r contains an English receipt.
The manifest description calls this one of the best manuscripts; it is not treated here as the earliest known witness of the work.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1239
entities.description: A narrow parchment 'holster book' containing Troilus and Criseyde on fols. 1r–62v and selections from the Canterbury Tales on fols. 63r–106r.

physical_description.extent: 107 folios, with three paper flyleaves at front and rear, an unfoliated blank parchment leaf after fol. 85, and a stub
notes: Four hands; the first three copy Troilus and Criseyde.
Two leaves are lost after fol. 3, removing Book I lines 379–630.
British Museum binding, gilt fore-edge; fol. 102 is torn and repaired.
Furnivall's right-hand column is not solely a transcription of this witness because explicitly marked lacunae are supplied from Additional MS 12044 and Harley MS 2392.

entities.label: British Library Additional MS 12044
entities.description: An imperfect parchment witness of Troilus and Criseyde used by Furnivall to supply selected gaps in the Harley 1239 column.

physical_description.extent: One medieval flyleaf and 113 folios, with two paper and one parchment flyleaves at front and one parchment and one paper flyleaf at rear
notes: Troilus and Criseyde occupies fols. 1r–113v and is imperfect at the end.
Three leaves are absent before fol. 113, removing Book V lines 1541–1750, and one leaf after fol. 113 removes lines 1826–1869.
Two scribes; green leather binding with the arms of Charles Butler.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2392
entities.description: A complete paper-and-parchment manuscript of Troilus and Criseyde used for one supplied stanza in Furnivall's Harley 1239 column.

physical_description.support: Paper and parchment, with outer and inner bifolia of parchment in predominantly twelve-leaf quires
physical_description.extent: 145 folios, with three paper and one parchment flyleaves at front and one parchment and three paper flyleaves at rear
notes: Troilus and Criseyde occupies fols. 1r–145v.
The scribe signs 'Style' on fol. 145v; the manuscript was rebound in 1964.

entities.label: Google Books scan of Three More Parallel Texts
entities.description: A complete 325-page PDF scan of a bound copy, including preliminaries, Macaulay's note, the complete 296 printed pages of the parallel text, and publisher matter.

entities.label: Parker Library digital facsimile of Corpus Christi College MS 61
entities.description: A 326-canvas IIIF surrogate including inside and outside boards, front and rear endleaves, fols. 1r–151v, and blank or non-work leaves exposed by the provider.
entities.reproduction_format: Full-color IIIF page and binding images
notes: Representative images 1, 16, 17, 313, and 326 were visually inspected after download; they show both boards, the illuminated frontispiece, text, and the work's ending in legible full color.
The provider exposes no separate spine or edge canvas, so the local bundle is complete relative to the published IIIF object, not every physically possible surface.

entities.label: 1983 printed facsimile of St John's College MS L.1
entities.description: A printed manuscript facsimile with an introduction by Richard Beadle and Jeremy J. Griffiths; its exact binding, flyleaf, blank-leaf, and exterior coverage was not verified from a copy.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, Troilus and Criseyde: A Facsimile of Cambridge, St John's College, MS L.1, introduction by Richard Beadle and Jeremy J. Griffiths (1983).
bibliographic.title: Troilus and Criseyde: A Facsimile of Cambridge, St John's College, MS L.1

notes: The catalog title and publication details are secure, but a physical copy must be checked before calling it a complete physical-object reproduction.

entities.label: M. R. James, Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in St John's College, Cambridge

bibliographic.citation: Montague Rhodes James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of St John's College, Cambridge (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1913), p. 274.
bibliographic.title: A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of St John's College, Cambridge

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
entities.description: Official guidance used only for the U.S. duration basis of the historical edition; it does not license modern reproductions.

agents.name: A. Rogers
notes: Identified by Furnivall as a member of Cambridge University Library staff who copied and collated the St John's and Corpus manuscripts; a fuller authorized form was not verified.

agents.name: Parker Library, Corpus Christi College, Cambridge

object.description: University of Michigan CME item CME00021

scope.notes: Identifiers, title-page transcription, structure, and text agree; the exact repository transfer date is not documented.

subject.description: The complete surviving U-M digital text

object.description: The edition title page and parallel poem through printed p. 294

scope.notes: The encoding serializes each printed three-column page as version 1 St John's, version 2 Corpus, and version 3 Harley. It omits the dedication, copying-and-collation note, Macaulay's essay, publisher lists, and printed pp. 295–296 (Book V lines 1835–1869) in all three columns.

subject.description: St John's L.1 parallel column, version 1 in each XML book

scope.notes: A. Rogers copied and collated this column; the later Testament of Cresseid is outside the transcribed work.

subject.description: Corpus MS 61 parallel column, version 2 in each XML book

scope.notes: A. Rogers copied and collated this column.

subject.description: Harley 1239 parallel column excluding explicitly supplied passages, version 3 in each XML book
locators: excluding Book I lines 379–630, 890–896, and 904–973; Book III lines 953–959 and 1387–1393; Book IV lines 953–1085 and 1149–1155; Book V lines 225–231

scope.notes: Furnivall copied the Harley manuscript. The excluded intervals are separately related to the witnesses that supply them.

subject.description: Explicitly marked supplied passages within the composite Harley 1239 column

locators: Troilus and Criseyde, fols. 1r–113v; exact folio crosswalk not yet verified

scope.notes: Only direct, bracketed or explicitly identified supplies are modeled; comparative notes that merely cite Additional MS 12044 are not treated as source substitution.

subject.description: One explicitly bracketed supplied stanza in the composite Harley 1239 column

locators: Troilus and Criseyde, fols. 1r–145v; exact folio crosswalk not yet verified

object.description: Bound combined issue, including preliminaries, Macaulay's note, poem pp. 1–296, and publisher matter

subject.description: All 326 canvases in the published IIIF Presentation manifest

object.description: Both boards, endleaves, fols. 1r–151v, blanks, and adjacent non-work matter represented by the provider

scope.notes: No separate spine or edge canvases are exposed.

subject.description: Published manuscript facsimile; exact leaf and exterior inventory not checked

object.description: St John's MS L.1; catalog title claims manuscript facsimile

assertion.notes: Do not infer complete physical-object coverage without inspecting a copy.

object.description: St John's MS L.1 as cataloged in 1913

rights.component: The CME00021 digital text and materials presented by University of Michigan
rights.basis: The U-M item page applies the No Copyright–United States rights statement.
notes: No Copyright–United States is a jurisdictional rights statement, not a worldwide license or a license for independently supplied manuscript photographs.

rights.component: The local XML copy corresponding to the U-M CME00021 encoding
rights.basis: The file is a supported copy of the U-M item whose provider applies No Copyright–United States; no independent expressive layer was identified in the local XML.

rights.component: The 1894–1895 edition, transcriptions, Furnivall and Rogers editorial work, and Macaulay note
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1894–1895, well before the U.S. public-domain cutoff described in Copyright Office Circular 15A.
notes: This status does not transfer to a modern scan service or manuscript photographs.

rights.component: Google Books scan files and delivery service surrounding a U.S.-public-domain edition
rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain in the United States, but no independent open license for Google's scan files or delivery terms was verified.

rights.component: The published Parker Library/Stanford digital images of Corpus Christi College MS 61
rights.basis: The IIIF manifest and MODS record explicitly license the published images under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International.
notes: Required attribution: Images courtesy of The Parker Library, Corpus Christi College, Cambridge.
The manifest directs users to parker-library@corpus.cam.ac.uk for higher-resolution images suitable for scholarly or commercial publication.

rights.component: Any modern reading-copy or high-resolution images supplied by St John's College
rights.basis: St John's distinguishes private-research copies from images for publication and requires permission for reproduction in publications, websites, or public presentations.

rights.component: The 1983 printed facsimile's photography, introduction, design, and edition
rights.basis: The catalog record identifies a 1983 publication and copyright notice; no open license was found.

rights.component: Any modern reference or reproduction images supplied through British Library Imaging Services
rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services warns that copyright may subsist and requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.
notes: The British Library catalog site's CC BY notice applies to site text, not automatically to manuscript images.

rights.component: Any modern reference or reproduction images supplied through British Library Imaging Services
rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services warns that copyright may subsist and requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.

rights.component: Any modern reference or reproduction images supplied through British Library Imaging Services
rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services warns that copyright may subsist and requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three independent manuscript texts with divergent readings and lacunae
editorial_practices.representation: St John's L.1, Corpus MS 61, and a composite Harley 1239 stream printed in aligned parallel columns with coordinated book, stanza, and line numbering
editorial_practices.description: The edition preserves three witness streams for comparison rather than constructing one silent eclectic text.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Physical and textual gaps in Harley MS 1239
editorial_practices.representation: Bracketed or annotated passages supplied from Additional MS 12044 and one stanza from Harley MS 2392 within the right-hand column
editorial_practices.description: The column heading alone is insufficient provenance: explicitly supplied passages have separate source relations.
editorial_practices.scope: Book I lines 379–630, 890–896, 904–973; Book III lines 953–959 and 1387–1393; Book IV lines 953–1085 and 1149–1155; Book V lines 225–231

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three parallel printed columns and substantial preliminary matter
editorial_practices.representation: Each book contains three sequential DIV3 version streams; most preliminaries and the Macaulay essay are omitted
editorial_practices.description: The XML order is a digital transformation of a synoptic page layout, not an intrinsic fifteen-part work structure.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The edition continues to Book V line 1869 and three explicit endings on printed pp. 295–296
editorial_practices.representation: All three encoded versions stop after line 1834 on printed p. 294
editorial_practices.description: The shared endpoint is an encoding omission, not a common textual lacuna in the three manuscripts.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00021.xml.
evidence.locator: Title page; Books I–V; fifteen DIV3 witness versions; opening, supply, and closing notes
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00021, Furnivall's edition, the three principal streams, printed-page and manuscript-leaf milestones, supplied-passage notes, and an encoded ending at Book V line 1834.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, 'Three more parallel texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde,' CME item CME00021.
evidence.summary: Confirms the item title, Chaucer attribution, 1894–1895 publication, public full-text access, stable identifier, and No Copyright–United States statement.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Three More Parallel Texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde (Chaucer Society, First Series 87–88, 1894–1895), Google Books U8IzAQAAMAAJ.
evidence.locator: Complete bound scan: title and responsibility leaves; Furnivall note dated 13 February 1896; Macaulay note; printed pp. 1–296
evidence.summary: Confirms the full bibliographic statement, division of copying and collation between Rogers and Furnivall, three-column layout, direct passage supplies, the Macaulay essay, and the complete ending through Book V line 1869 on pp. 295–296.

evidence.citation: HathiTrust Digital Library, record 011665171, Three More Parallel Texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde.
evidence.summary: Catalogs two volumes dated 1894–1895, Chaucer Society First Series 87–88, 26 × 34 cm, with Cornell item coo.31924013111343 and public-domain status.

evidence.citation: Middle English Compendium, Bibliography entry BIB625, 'Troilus and Criseyde.'
evidence.locator: Entry info; witness stencils; Furnivall edition entry
evidence.summary: Supplies c. 1385 work dating, Middle English classification, the Furnivall citation, and modern designations for Corpus 61, Harley 1239, and St John's L.1.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of St John's College, Cambridge (1913), p. 274.
evidence.summary: Describes MS L.1 as a parchment Troilus manuscript ending on fol. 119v with the later Testament of Cresseid following; supplies dimensions and an older broad date.

evidence.citation: Late Medieval English Scribes, manuscript record for Cambridge, St John's College MS L.1.
evidence.summary: Records date 1425–1450, parchment support, current foliation and dimensions, Troilus ending on fol. 119v, later Testament, 1683 acquisition, and selected scholarly images.

evidence.citation: St John's College, Cambridge, Old Library research highlights.
evidence.summary: Confirms that the college holds an early fifteenth-century manuscript of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde among its special collections.

evidence.citation: St John's College, Cambridge, Library FAQs, image requests and Special Collections consultation.
evidence.locator: Questions 'Can I reproduce or request images' and 'How do I make an appointment'
evidence.summary: States that reading copies and high-resolution publication images may be requested, public reproduction needs college permission, and onsite consultation is by appointment under supervision.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 45848, Troilus and Criseyde: A Facsimile of Cambridge, St John's College, MS L.1 (1983).
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1983 printed facsimile, introduction by Richard Beadle and Jeremy J. Griffiths, approximate extent, and copyright notice; exact leaf and exterior coverage remains unchecked.

evidence.citation: Parker Library on the Web / Stanford Libraries, Cambridge, Corpus Christi College, MS 061.
evidence.summary: Provides shelfmark, date, 151-folio extent, dimensions, support, foliation, layout, contents on fols. 1v–150r, ownership and receipt leaves, and explicit CC BY-NC 4.0 image terms.

evidence.citation: Parker Library / Stanford Libraries, IIIF Presentation manifest for Corpus Christi College MS 61.
evidence.locator: 326 canvases; work range canvas 16 (fol. 1v) through canvas 313 (fol. 150r)
evidence.summary: Exposes the complete published digital object, all canvas labels and image services, a structured 298-canvas work range, attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0 license.
notes: The SHA-256 identifies the locally built ZIP bundle containing this manifest, inventory, and 326 downloaded images, not the remote JSON alone.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of cached Parker Library IIIF bundle for Corpus Christi College MS 61.
evidence.summary: Representative beginning, work-opening, text-opening, work-ending, and final-binding images are legible full-color captures; both boards and the complete work endpoints match the manifest labels.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 1239, catalog record 040-002047068.
evidence.summary: Records the 1450–1474 parchment holster book, complete physical extent and dimensions, Troilus on fols. 1r–62v, lost leaves after fol. 3, adjacent Canterbury Tales selections, binding details, and a digital viewer whose images are currently unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Additional MS 12044, catalog record 040-002041751.
evidence.summary: Records a 1425–1449 parchment Troilus witness on fols. 1r–113v, its complete physical extent and binding, missing leaves and final imperfection, and a digital viewer whose images are currently unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 2392, catalog record 040-002048223.
evidence.summary: Records a 1425–1474 paper-and-parchment Troilus witness on fols. 1r–145v, its full extent, construction, dimensions, binding, signed scribe, access condition, and a digital viewer whose images are currently unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.summary: Describes online ordering for collection images, required shelfmark and folio details, reference versus reproduction images, photography rather than scanning for original manuscripts, color requests, fees, and separate permissions concerns.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services price list.
evidence.summary: Provides current indicative charges while noting that large or unusual complete-manuscript requests may require quotation.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.locator: Preservation of originals; pricing; copyright and reproductions
evidence.summary: States quality and format conditions, quotation and turnaround qualifications, responsibility for third-party rights, and the need for written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Official guidance used to support the U.S. public-domain status of an edition published in 1894–1895; it does not determine rights in modern scan files or photographs.

evidence.citation: Public complete-facsimile search for CME00021 source witnesses, 2026-07-11.
evidence.summary: Checks of St John's College, Late Medieval English Scribes, Cambridge Digital Library, Biblissima/IIIF, British Library current catalog and digital item links, and Internet Archive found no publicly usable complete digital surrogate for St John's L.1, Harley MS 1239, Additional MS 12044, or Harley MS 2392. The British Library catalog explicitly marks all three digital image sets unavailable.

open_questions.question: Was the shared omission of printed pp. 295–296 and Book V lines 1835–1869 intentional in the original U-M encoding or an accidental truncation?
next_steps: Consult U-M production documentation or report the endpoint discrepancy to the provider before repairing the source XML.
If a correction is authorized, transcribe all three final columns from the complete scan and preserve their distinct explicits.

open_questions.question: Does the combined title leaf in the Google scan represent the original two-part issue, a later bound issue, or a title leaf reset after Furnivall's 13 February 1896 note?
next_steps: Compare separately issued wrappers and title leaves for Chaucer Society First Series 87 and 88 in institutional copies.
Do not assign a post-1895 publication date solely from the dated copying note.

open_questions.question: Can St John's College supply or license a complete present-object digital reproduction of MS L.1, including all bindings, flyleaves, blanks, and the adjacent Testament of Cresseid?
next_steps: Request a whole-object research quotation from special.collections@joh.cam.ac.uk.
Inspect the 1983 printed facsimile's leaf and exterior inventory before using it as a fallback.

open_questions.question: Will the British Library restore public images or quote complete-object photography for Harley MS 1239, Additional MS 12044, and Harley MS 2392?
next_steps: Submit separate complete-object imaging requests with all bindings, flyleaves, blanks, stubs, pastedowns, adjacent texts, spine, and edges specified.
Obtain written reuse terms separately from the supply quotation.

open_questions.question: What exact manuscript folios correspond to every passage Furnivall supplies from Additional MS 12044 and Harley MS 2392?
next_steps: Establish folio crosswalks from restored public images or complete research reproductions.
Recheck bracket boundaries against the print scan before using the passage list for automated collation.
